steps.specialNote: قَلْب derives from 'to turn over' — the heart is named for its constant turning. قَلْبٌ سَلِيمٌ (a sound/pure heart) is the goal of the believer according to the Quran.

ayahCards.arabicText: إِلَّا مَنْ أَتَى اللَّهَ بِقَلْبٍ سَلِيمٍ
ayahCards.translation: But only one who comes to Allah with a sound heart.

ayahCards.arabicText: فِي قُلُوبِهِم مَّرَضٌ فَزَادَهُمُ اللَّهُ مَرَضًا
ayahCards.translation: In their hearts is disease, so Allah has increased their disease.

steps.question: In رَبِّ الْعَالَمِينَ, what type of construction is this?
choices: Idhafah — رَبِّ is mudaf, الْعَالَمِينَ is mudaf ilayhi
steps.explanation: Idhafah (possessive construction): رَبِّ = 'Lord of' (first word/mudaf — loses tanwin, takes kasra here because it follows a preposition), الْعَالَمِينَ = 'the worlds' (second word/mudaf ilayhi — always majroor).

steps.question: مَالِكِ يَوْمِ الدِّينِ contains two Idhafah constructions. Which analysis is correct?
choices: مَالِكِ يَوْمِ = first Idhafah; يَوْمِ الدِّينِ = second Idhafah (chained)
All three words are in Idhafah with each other simultaneously
Only مَالِكِ الدِّينِ is Idhafah; يَوْمِ is separate
steps.explanation: Idhafah can be chained: مَالِكِ يَوْمِ الدِّينِ = 'Master of [the Day of Judgement]'. يَوْم is both the mudaf ilayhi of مَالِك AND the mudaf of الدِّين — a beautiful nested construction.

steps.verseArabic: إِلَّا مَنْ أَتَى اللَّهَ بِ___ سَلِيمٍ
steps.verseTranslation: But only one who comes to Allah with a sound ___.

steps.question: What is the 3-letter root of الْعَالَمِينَ?
